dateline: New Delhi / Islamabad / Srinagar / United Nations — August 1947–Present
heroHeadline: Indian Army Launches Pre-Sindoor Anniversary Campaign: 'Outcomes That Endure' — 9 Terror Camps; Pakistan's Munir Meets Iranian FM as Diplomatic Isolation of India Widens
heroSubtitle: On April 26, 2026 — eleven days before the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor — the Indian Army posted 'Precision driven. Terror Targeting. Outcomes that Endure,' displaying the nine terror camps destroyed in the May 2025 strikes and warning 'India does not forget.' The April 26 message was the second in a coordinated pre-anniversary deterrence campaign, following the April 25 'New normal, new posture' post. Meanwhile, Field Marshal Asim Munir met Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi on April 25, continuing Pakistan's high-tempo regional diplomacy that began with its US-Iran ceasefire mediation (April 8), cementing Islamabad's post-Sindoor status as an indispensable regional peace broker while India remains on the diplomatic sidelines. India's NIA also attached a property in Budgam in a Pakistan-linked terror financing case on April 25. The bilateral standoff holds: ceasefire technically in place since May 2025, airspace closed, Indus Waters Treaty suspended, UNSC letter filed — and the Sindoor anniversary approaching on May 7.
footerNote: Data sourced from Indian MEA/MOD, Pakistan ISPR/MFA, UN Security Council, SIPRI, Crisis Group, HRW, Stimson Center
